meta.label: Moi Moi
meta.description: Nigerian moi moi (moin moin): steamed bean pudding made from peeled black-eyed peas blended with pepper, onions, and seasoning, then steamed until set. Often includes fish, egg, or liver.

source.author: Suka Smart Assistant
source.reference: Classic moi moi method using peeled beans; standardized for SSA catalog.

summary: Classic Nigerian moi moi: smooth blended black-eyed peas with pepper and onions, lightly enriched with oil, then steamed in cups until set—perfect alongside rice, pap, or as a protein-rich snack.

notes: Naturally gluten-free and dairy-free; verify bouillon if used.
Steam until fully set (center not wet).
If adding fish, remove bones before serving.

ingredients.label: Black-eyed peas (peeled/skinless)
notes: If using unpeeled dry beans, soak and rub skins off; peeled beans blend smoother.

ingredients.label: Water for blending (or light stock)
notes: Add gradually to reach a thick but pourable batter.

ingredients.label: Scotch bonnet or habanero
notes: About 1 small pepper; reduce for mild.

ingredients.label: Neutral oil (vegetable/canola) or palm oil
notes: Palm oil gives traditional color and flavor; neutral oil is lighter.

ingredients.label: Salt
notes: Add to taste depending on bouillon and mix-ins.

ingredients.label: Bouillon powder or cube (crumbled)
notes: Optional; common in many versions.

ingredients.label: Hard-boiled eggs, quartered
notes: Optional; 1–2 pieces per portion.

ingredients.label: Canned sardines or mackerel, drained
notes: Optional; flake into chunks.

ingredients.label: Beef liver, parboiled and diced small
notes: Optional; parboil 8–10 minutes then dice.

ingredients.label: Ramekins, heat-safe cups, or foil packs
notes: Traditional: banana leaves; modern: foil packs or small cups.
A high-speed blender yields the smoothest moi moi.
You can steam in a large pot with a rack or folded towel at the bottom.

steps.title: Set up the steamer
steps.body: Fill a large pot with 2–3 inches of water. Place a steamer rack or a folded towel at the bottom so cups don’t sit directly on the pot base. Bring to a gentle simmer while you blend the batter.

steps.title: Blend pepper-onion mixture
steps.body: Blend red bell pepper, scotch bonnet (to taste), and onion with about 250 ml of the water until very smooth.

steps.title: Blend beans until smooth
steps.body: Add peeled black-eyed peas to the blender. Blend, adding remaining water gradually, until a smooth, thick, pourable batter forms. Work in batches if needed.

steps.title: Season and enrich the batter
steps.body: Pour batter into a mixing bowl. Stir in oil, salt, bouillon (optional), and optional curry/thyme. Mix well for 1–2 minutes to aerate slightly; taste for seasoning and adjust.
notes: Batter should be pourable like a thick pancake batter—add small splashes of water if too thick.

steps.title: Fill cups and add mix-ins
steps.body: Lightly oil cups/ramekins or prepare foil packs. Ladle batter to fill each about 3/4 full. Add mix-ins (egg piece, fish chunk, diced liver) into the center of each portion if using, then top with a little more batter as needed.

steps.title: Steam until set
steps.body: Arrange cups in the steamer. Cover the pot (wrap lid with a towel if you want to reduce condensation dripping). Steam on medium heat until set and a tester comes out mostly clean, 45–60 minutes depending on cup size. Add more hot water to the pot if level gets low.

steps.title: Rest and serve
steps.body: Let moi moi rest 5 minutes, then serve warm. Enjoy with rice/stew, pap (ogi), bread, or as a snack. Cool and refrigerate leftovers up to 4 days.

notes: Texture key: use peeled beans and blend very smooth for classic custardy moi moi.
Water control: batter should pour; too much water makes soft/wet centers.
Condensation tip: wrap the lid with a clean towel to keep drips from pooling on top.
Flavor options: palm oil adds traditional color; neutral oil keeps it lighter.
Serving ideas: pairs well with jollof rice, fried rice, stew, or pap (ogi).

variations.label: Banana-leaf wrapped
notes: Line cups or wrap in softened banana leaves for classic aroma; steam as directed.

variations.label: Mild / kid-friendly
notes: Use only a small piece of scotch bonnet or omit; add more red bell pepper for flavor without heat.

variations.label: Fish-focused
notes: Use flaked mackerel/sardines and omit liver/egg; ensure bones are removed.
